chunks.text: At the end of 2019, in Wuhan, China, a new virus was discovered.
Within a few weeks, it had spread across borders to the rest of the world.
In March 2020, the World Health Organization declared it a pandemic.
Countries imposed lockdown, and schools and many businesses were closed.
Infected patient were carried to hospital across each region.
Intensive care units quickly filled up.
Doctors and nurses worked without regard to their own risk.
Symptoms differed from one person to another.
Fever, cough, and shortness of breath were common.
but elderly people and those with existing diseases became seriously ill more easily.
Researchers developed a vaccine at an unprecedented speed.
Within just over a year, several vaccines were approved, and vaccination began throughout the world.
The pandemic has left us many lesson.
How vital public health preparedness is.
How without international cooperation, infectious disease cannot be stopped.
To prepare for the next crisis, we must not forget these lessons.
